prompt: In a OSPF environment, investigate adjacency stuck. Explain scope, dependencies, ranked hypotheses, tests, evidence, corrective action, validation, and prevention.

answer_framework.scope: Identify affected users, hosts, VLANs, sites, protocols, and the exact failure window.
answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ss -tulpn and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed intermittent packet loss is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with ping and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show interfaces trunk and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to OSPF is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with nslookup and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.
